subject: Re: Question about tilt series dose in warp
body:
Hi Linhua,

Just a guess but I assume if the pre-exposure does is 0 e/A2 and post exposure is 3 e/A2 then the average dose is 1.5 e/A2 and thus the dose filtering is applied according to this? This would mean that maybe the first few frames are over filtered and the last few are under filtered but I imagine the final difference is very small!

Best,

Alister

> On 27 Mar 2020, at 12:10, Linhua Tai <8883669@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> ﻿
> 
> Dear all,
> Hi, I'm using a dose symmetric scheme, and after import imod aligned tilt series back to warp, and set the dose to 3 e/A, which is the dose at 0 degree, I found out in fourier space tab, the dose at 0 degree is 1.5e and increase by 4.5, 7.5, 10.5 etc.
> The order is right for my tilt scheme, but the dose isn't right.
> 
> So my question is, what does the dose value stand for? Is it pre-exposure dose, or post-exposure dose?
> 
> Should I need to change the value in *.tomostar files to match the real case?
> 
> Thank you very much in advance!
> 
> Yours sincerely,
> Linhua Tai
> --
